briefdescription.para: Return the length of the data in the Receive Buffer.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.

simplesect.para: The type/length field of the frame received.

detaileddescription.para:
The initialization entails:
Initialize fields of the XEmacLite instance structure.
The driver defaults to polled mode operation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XEmacLite instance.
points to the XEmacLite device configuration structure.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. If the address translation is not used then the physical address is passed. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

simplesect.para: The initialization of the PHY device is not done in this function. The user needs to use XEmacLite_PhyRead and XEmacLite_PhyWrite functions to access the PHY device.

detaileddescription.para: The ByteCount is the total frame size, including header.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XEmacLite instance.
FramePtr
is a pointer to frame. For optimal performance, a 32-bit aligned buffer should be used but it is not required, the function will align the data if necessary.
ByteCount
is the size, in bytes, of the frame
XST_SUCCESS if data was transmitted.
XST_FAILURE if buffer(s) was (were) full and no valid data was transmitted.
This function call is not blocking in nature, i.e. it will not wait until the frame is transmitted.
Intended to be called from the interrupt context or with a wrapper which waits for the receive frame to be available.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XEmacLite instance.
FramePtr
is a pointer to a buffer where the frame will be stored. The buffer must be at least XEL_MAX_FRAME_SIZE bytes. For optimal performance, a 32-bit aligned buffer should be used but it is not required, the function will align the data if necessary.
The type/length field of the frame received. When the type/length field contains the type, XEL_MAX_FRAME_SIZE bytes will be copied out of the buffer and it is up to the higher layers to sort out the frame. Function returns 0 if there is no data waiting in the receive buffer or the pong buffer if configured.
This function call is not blocking in nature, i.e. it will not wait until a frame arrives.

briefdescription.para: Set the MAC address for this device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XEmacLite instance.
is a pointer to a 6-byte MAC address. the format of the MAC address is major octet to minor octet

para.verbatim: - TX must be idle and RX should be idle for deterministic results. It is recommended that this function should be called after the initialization and before transmission of any packets from the device. - Function will not return if hardware is absent or not functioning properly. - The MAC address can be programmed using any of the two transmit buffers (if configured).

briefdescription.para: This is a stub for the send and receive callbacks.

detaileddescription.para: The stub is here in case the upper layers forget to set the handlers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the upper layer callback reference.

briefdescription.para: Determine if there is a transmit buffer available.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the instance of the driver to be worked on.

listitem.para: TRUE if there is a TX buffer available for data to be written
FALSE if Tx Buffer is not available.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the instance of the driver to be worked on.
is the pointer to the instance of the driver.
is the address of the PHY device. The valid range is is from 0 to 31.
is the register number in the PHY device which is to be read. The valid range is is from 0 to 31.
is a pointer to the data in which the data read from the PHY device is returned.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the data is read from the PHY.

simplesect.para: This function waits for the completion of MDIO data transfer.

memberdef.argsstring: (XEmacLite *InstancePtr, u32 PhyAddress, u32 RegNum, u16 PhyData)
memberdef.name: XEmacLite_PhyWrite

briefdescription.para: Write the given data to the specified register in the PHY device.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the instance of the driver.
is the address of the PHY device. The valid range is is from 0 to 31.
is the register number in the PHY device which is to be written. The valid range is is from 0 to 31.
is the data to be written to the specified register in the PHY device.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the data is written to the PHY.

simplesect.para: This function waits for the completion of MDIO data transfer.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the instance of the driver.
is the pointer to the instance of the driver.
